name: Route of the Quijote by the Peñarroya Reservoir — circular through the Natural Park Lagunas de Ruidera
name_original: Ruta del Quijote por el Embalse Peñarroya — circular por el Parque Natural Lagunas de Ruidera
region_intro: Hiking around Ruidera offers access to the distinctive Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, characterized by a system of fifteen interconnected lagoons. These lagoons are linked by waterfalls and streams, formed by natural travertine barriers. The landscape transitions from arid steppe to lush areas with diverse vegetation, creating a unique microclimate. Best hiking trails around Ruidera The most popular hiking route is Lagunas del Rey y de la Colgada — circular por el Parque Natural Lagunas de Ruidera, an 8.7 mile (14.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes through several of the park's key lagoons. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lagunas de Ruidera Circular Route, a difficult 12.5 mile (20.1 km) path. This route explores the interconnected lagoon system and its natural waterfalls. Local hikers also love the Route of the Quijote by the Peñarroya Reservoir — circular through the Natural Park Lagunas de Ruidera, a 12.5 mile (20.1 km) trail leading through the reservoir area, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Ruidera is defined by interconnected lagoons, natural waterfalls, and diverse vegetation, ranging from marshy areas to woodlands. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Ruidera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 160 reviews. Nearly 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Ruidera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ruidera?
A: There are over a dozen hiking routes available in Ruidera, offering a variety of experiences within the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park. These routes range from easy walks to more challenging trails, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Ruidera?
A: Yes, Ruidera offers several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. The park is known for its gentle walks through wooded areas and along the lagoons. For example, the region research mentions an easy interpretive route around the Tomilla and Conceja lagoons, and a popular 14 km circular route suitable for families starting at El Hundimiento waterfall.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park?
A: While the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park is a beautiful place to explore, it's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect the diverse wildlife and natural environment. Always check the specific regulations of the park or individual trails before heading out with your pet.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in Ruidera?
A: The Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. Along the trails, you can observe a variety of flora and fauna, including colorful birds, making it an exceptional place for bird watching. The lush areas around the lagoons support diverse vegetation like great fen-sedge, reeds, and various trees such as poplars, fig trees, and willows.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Ruidera?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular way to experience the park. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198645177/lagunas-del-rey-y-de-la-colgada-circular-por-el-parque-natural-lagunas-de-ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas del Rey y de la Colgada — circular por el Parque Natural Lagunas de Ruidera&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.7-mile route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084132473/lagunas-de-ruidera-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Ruidera Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a more challenging 12.5-mile path exploring the interconnected lagoon system.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of hiking routes in Ruidera?
A: Hiking routes in Ruidera vary in length and duration. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198645177/lagunas-del-rey-y-de-la-colgada-circular-por-el-parque-natural-lagunas-de-ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas del Rey y de la Colgada — circular por el Parque Natural Lagunas de Ruidera&lt;/a&gt; is 8.7 miles (14 km) and takes about 3 hours 43 minutes. Longer routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199577968/route-of-the-quijote-by-the-penarroya-reservoir-circular-through-the-natural-park-lagunas-de-ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Quijote by the Peñarroya Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, are around 12.5 miles (20.1 km) and can take over 5 hours.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the hiking trails in Ruidera?
A: The trails in Ruidera offer access to many natural and historical attractions. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, known for its clear, turquoise waters. Historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2719681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the ruins of Rochafrida Castle. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanas Lagoon Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the natural area of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3019640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ossero Ford&lt;/a&gt;, part of an easy interpretive route.

Q: Can I swim in the lagoons after a hike?
A: Yes, the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park is popular for swimming. Many visitors enjoy taking a refreshing dip in the clear waters of the lagoons, especially during warmer months. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey&lt;/a&gt; is particularly well-known for its accessible location and inviting waters.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ruidera?
A: The hiking experience in Ruidera is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique landscape of interconnected lagoons and waterfalls, the diverse vegetation, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities. The picturesque views and the opportunity to connect with nature are frequently highlighted.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking trails in Ruidera?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to all trailheads might be limited, Ruidera is generally accessible. Visitors often travel by car to reach the natural park, where various parking options are available near popular starting points for hikes. It's advisable to check local transport schedules if relying on public options.

Q: When is the best time of year to hike in Ruidera?
A: The Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, but the opportunity for swimming in the lagoons makes it an attractive option. Winter offers a quieter experience, though some facilities might have reduced hours.
tips: Beautiful Parish in Ruidera, Nuestra Señora de la Blanca.

The Peñarroya Reservoir, like many others across Spain, was built during the Franco dictatorship. Its main purpose is to supply drinking water to nearby towns and irrigate the fields of La Mancha. It is also well-known among fishing enthusiasts who flock to the reservoir's shores every weekend in search of a photograph-worthy catch.
In summer, the Peñarroya Reservoir is the second most popular bathing destination within the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park.

This is the Sinking Waterfall that remained as a result of the natural modification that occurred in the middle of the 16th century in the upstream retention dam. This modification seems to have taken place in relation to the high rainfall that occurred throughout Spain and that led to the "sinking" of part of that natural rock dam that originally existed.
